global.title: The Bulletin: May 15’s News in 60 seconds - Newsweek
global.summary: Trump and Xi ended their Beijing summit with warm rhetoric but few concrete breakthroughs, while the Iran war continues to pressure oil supplies and raise concern about fuel costs.
global.whyItMatters: This helps frame prayer and preaching around global instability, economic anxiety, and the limits of political power.

global.title: Week Ahead for FX, Bonds: Fed Minutes in Focus as Middle East Uncertainty Continues - WSJ
global.summary: Markets are watching for signs of possible rate pressure as elevated energy prices and Middle East uncertainty cloud the outlook for growth and inflation.
global.whyItMatters: Economic stress often shows up first in family strain, generosity pressures, and pastoral care needs.

global.title: Five Italian divers killed in cave dive in the Maldives - NBC News
global.summary: Five Italian divers died during a cave dive in the Maldives, a sobering reminder of how suddenly tragedy can unfold.
global.whyItMatters: Pastors regularly help people process grief, fragility, and the brevity of life; stories like this sharpen that ministry lens.

canadian.title: Canada Accelerates Mining Reform With 1-Year Approval Cap, Sending Resource Stocks Higher - NAI500
canadian.summary: Ottawa has launched consultations on reforms that would cap federal review and decision timelines for mining and infrastructure projects at one year after complete applications are filed.
canadian.whyItMatters: Resource policy shapes jobs, regional confidence, environmental debate, and the social pressures churches will pastor through in Canada.

canadian.title: Non-Jewish professor says he was fired for calling out Hamas supporters in online post - Fox News
canadian.summary: A Canadian professor says he lost his job after defending Israel online, as the story is set against a wider backdrop of rising antisemitism and intimidation in Canada.
canadian.whyItMatters: Pastors should be alert to growing fear, polarization, and the need to model both courage and charity in public witness.

persecutedChurch.title: Ihab Hassan: ‘No accountability’ over attacks on Palestinian Christians - Vatican News
persecutedChurch.summary: Vatican News highlights concerns from Palestinian Christians in the West Bank who say attacks by settlers continue without meaningful accountability.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: It invites informed prayer for vulnerable believers in the land of Jesus and helps congregations remember the church beyond their own setting.

persecutedChurch.title: Blessed Sacrament stolen from Nigerian adoration chapel - Catholic World Report
persecutedChurch.summary: A reported theft from a Nigerian chapel comes amid staggering figures on violence against Christians in Nigeria, where church groups say killings and attacks remain severe.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: Nigeria remains one of the most urgent places for intercession, awareness, and solidarity with suffering Christians.

leadership.title: Two Case Studies Reveal 4 Lessons That Leadership Is About Conviction Not Survival - Forbes
leadership.summary: Forbes argues that durable leadership requires conviction, clear identity, and attention to well-being rather than living in constant survival mode.
leadership.whyItMatters: A lead pastor needs sustainable rhythms; this is a useful prompt to lead from calling instead of exhaustion.

goodNews.title: Community fundraises $57K to enable a Howard University senior to graduate on time - NBC News
goodNews.summary: A community rallied to raise $57,000 so a Howard University senior could graduate on time, offering a tangible picture of shared generosity.
goodNews.whyItMatters: It is a hopeful example of burden-bearing love that can encourage a church toward practical compassion.
